arab: إِنَّمَا الأَعْمَالُ بِالنِّيَّاتِ، وَإِنَّمَا لِكُلِّ امْرِئٍ مَا نَوَى، فَمَنْ كَانَتْ هِجْرَتُهُ إِلَى اللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ فَهِجْرَتُهُ إِلَى اللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ، وَمَنْ كَانَتْ هِجْرَتُهُ لِدُنْيَا يُصِيبُهَا أَوِ امْرَأَةٍ يَنْكِحُهَا فَهِجْرَتُهُ إِلَى مَا هَاجَرَ إِلَيْهِ
en: Actions are judged by intentions, and every person will have what they intended. So whoever migrates for Allah and His Messenger, his migration is for Allah and His Messenger. And whoever migrates for worldly gain or to marry a woman, his migration is for what he migrated to.
lesson: The foundation of Islam. Check your niyyah before every deed. Are you praying for Allah or to show off? Sincerity is everything.

arab: بَيْنَمَا نَحْنُ جُلُوسٌ عِنْدَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ ذَاتَ يَوْمٍ إِذْ طَلَعَ عَلَيْنَا رَجُلٌ شَدِيدُ بَيَاضِ الثِّيَابِ شَدِيدُ سَوَادِ الشَّعَرِ... قَالَ: فَأَخْبِرْنِي عَنِ الإِسْلاَمِ. قَالَ: الإِسْلاَمُ أَنْ تَشْهَدَ أَنْ لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَأَنَّ مُحَمَّدًا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ، وَتُقِيمَ الصَّلاَةَ، وَتُؤْتِيَ الزَّكَاةَ، وَتَصُومَ رَمَضَانَ، وَتَحُجَّ الْبَيْتَ إِنِ اسْتَطَعْتَ إِلَيْهِ سَبِيلاً
en: While we were sitting with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ one day, a man appeared with very white clothes and very black hair... He said: 'Tell me about Islam.' He said: 'Islam is to testify there is no god but Allah and Muhammad is His Messenger, to establish prayer, give zakah, fast Ramadan, and perform Hajj to the House if you can find a way.'
lesson: This is Hadith Jibril. Angel Jibril came to teach us Islam, Iman, and Ihsan. The 5 pillars are the foundation of a Muslim's life.

arab: بُنِيَ الإِسْلاَمُ عَلَى خَمْسٍ: شَهَادَةِ أَنْ لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَأَنَّ مُحَمَّدًا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ، وَإِقَامِ الصَّلاَةِ، وَإِيتَاءِ الزَّكَاةِ، وَحَجِّ الْبَيْتِ، وَصَوْمِ رَمَضَانَ
en: Islam is built upon five: Testifying there is no god but Allah and Muhammad is His Messenger, establishing prayer, giving zakah, Hajj to the House, and fasting Ramadan.
lesson: The 5 pillars hold up Islam like pillars hold up a building. If one breaks, the whole structure is in danger.

arab: إِنَّ أَحَدَكُمْ يُجْمَعُ خَلْقُهُ فِي بَطْنِ أُمِّهِ أَرْبَعِينَ يَوْمًا نُطْفَةً، ثُمَّ يَكُونُ عَلَقَةً مِثْلَ ذَلِكَ، ثُمَّ يَكُونُ مُضْغَةً مِثْلَ ذَلِكَ، ثُمَّ يُرْسَلُ إِلَيْهِ الْمَلَكُ فَيَنْفُخُ فِيهِ الرُّوحَ
en: Verily, the creation of each one of you is brought together in his mother's womb for forty days as a drop, then he is a clot for a similar period, then a morsel for a similar period, then the angel is sent to him and breathes the soul into him.
lesson: Allah decrees your provision, lifespan, deeds, and final destination while you're in the womb. Trust Allah's plan and work for good deeds.

arab: مَنْ أَحْدَثَ فِي أَمْرِنَا هَذَا مَا لَيْسَ مِنْهُ فَهُوَ رَدٌّ
en: Whoever introduces into this affair of ours something that does not belong to it, it is rejected.
lesson: No bid'ah in Islam. Worship must be based on Qur'an and Sunnah. Don't invent new ways of worship that the Prophet ﷺ didn't teach.

arab: إِنَّ الْحَلاَلَ بَيِّنٌ وَإِنَّ الْحَرَامَ بَيِّنٌ وَبَيْنَهُمَا أُمُورٌ مُشْتَبِهَاتٌ لاَ يَعْلَمُهُنَّ كَثِيرٌ مِنَ النَّاسِ، فَمَنِ اتَّقَى الشُّبُهَاتِ فَقَدِ اسْتَبْرَأَ لِدِينِهِ وَعِرْضِهِ
en: The lawful is clear and the unlawful is clear, and between them are doubtful matters that many people do not know about. So whoever avoids the doubtful matters has cleared himself regarding his religion and honor.
lesson: If you're not sure if something is halal, leave it. Protect your deen by staying away from grey areas. The heart is the king — keep it pure.

arab: الدِّينُ النَّصِيحَةُ. قُلْنَا: لِمَنْ؟ قَالَ: لِلَّهِ وَلِكِتَابِهِ وَلِرَسُولِهِ وَلأَئِمَّةِ الْمُسْلِمِينَ وَعَامَّتِهِمْ
en: The religion is sincere advice. We said: 'To whom?' He said: 'To Allah, His Book, His Messenger, the leaders of the Muslims, and their common people.'
lesson: Naseehah means wanting good for others. Correct your brother privately, not publicly. Sincerity to Allah, the Qur'an, and the Ummah.

arab: أُمِرْتُ أَنْ أُقَاتِلَ النَّاسَ حَتَّى يَشْهَدُوا أَنْ لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَأَنَّ مُحَمَّدًا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ، وَيُقِيمُوا الصَّلاَةَ، وَيُؤْتُوا الزَّكَاةَ
en: I have been commanded to fight people until they testify there is no god but Allah and Muhammad is His Messenger, establish prayer, and give zakah.
lesson: This refers to combatant enemies in war, not peaceful non-Muslims. Islam protects life. Context matters — study with scholars.

arab: مَا نَهَيْتُكُمْ عَنْهُ فَاجْتَنِبُوهُ، وَمَا أَمَرْتُكُمْ بِهِ فَأْتُوا مِنْهُ مَا اسْتَطَعْتُمْ
en: What I have forbidden you, avoid it. What I have ordered you to do, do as much of it as you can.
lesson: Avoiding haram is absolute. But for commands, do your best. Allah doesn't burden a soul beyond its capacity.

arab: إِنَّ اللَّهَ طَيِّبٌ لاَ يَقْبَلُ إِلاَّ طَيِّبًا، وَإِنَّ اللَّهَ أَمَرَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ بِمَا أَمَرَ بِهِ الْمُرْسَلِينَ
en: Allah is Good and accepts only what is good. Allah commanded the believers with what He commanded the Messengers.
lesson: Your income must be halal for your du'a to be accepted. Eat halal, earn halal, wear halal. Pure input = pure output.

arab: دَعْ مَا يَرِيبُكَ إِلَى مَا لاَ يَرِيبُكَ، فَإِنَّ الصِّدْقَ طُمَأْنِينَةٌ وَإِنَّ الْكَذِبَ رِيبَةٌ
en: Leave what makes you doubt for what does not make you doubt. Truthfulness is tranquility and lying is doubt.
lesson: Your heart knows. If something feels off, leave it. Truth brings peace to the heart, lies bring anxiety.

arab: مِنْ حُسْنِ إِسْلاَمِ الْمَرْءِ تَرْكُهُ مَا لاَ يَعْنِيهِ
en: Part of the perfection of a person's Islam is his leaving that which does not concern him.
lesson: Mind your own business. Don't spy, gossip, or get involved in drama. Focus on your own akhirah and leave people's affairs.

arab: لاَ يُؤْمِنُ أَحَدُكُمْ حَتَّى يُحِبَّ لأَخِيهِ مَا يُحِبُّ لِنَفْسِهِ
en: None of you truly believes until he loves for his brother what he loves for himself.
lesson: Want Jannah? Want good health? Want success? Want it for your Muslim brother too. This kills jealousy and builds brotherhood.

arab: لاَ يَحِلُّ دَمُ امْرِئٍ مُسْلِمٍ إِلاَّ بِإِحْدَى ثَلاَثٍ: الثَّيِّبُ الزَّانِي، وَالنَّفْسُ بِالنَّفْسِ، وَالتَّارِكُ لِدِينِهِ الْمُفَارِقُ لِلْجَمَاعَةِ
en: The blood of a Muslim is not lawful except in three cases: the married adulterer, a life for a life, and the one who abandons his religion and separates from the community.
lesson: Islam protects life. These punishments are only by Islamic court with strict evidence. No vigilantism in Islam.

arab: مَنْ كَانَ يُؤْمِنُ بِاللَّهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الآخِرِ فَلْيَقُلْ خَيْرًا أَوْ لِيَصْمُتْ، وَمَنْ كَانَ يُؤْمِنُ بِاللَّهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الآخِرِ فَلْيُكْرِمْ جَارَهُ
en: Whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day should speak good or remain silent, and whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day should honor his neighbor.
lesson: Control your tongue and be good to neighbors. These are signs of real Iman. Your neighbor has rights even if he's not Muslim.

arab: أَنَّ رَجُلاً قَالَ لِلنَّبِيِّ ﷺ: أَوْصِنِي. قَالَ: لاَ تَغْضَبْ. فَرَدَّدَ مِرَارًا، قَالَ: لاَ تَغْضَبْ
en: A man said to the Prophet ﷺ: 'Advise me.' He said: 'Do not become angry.' The man repeated his request several times, and he said: 'Do not become angry.'
lesson: Anger destroys. When angry: say 'A'udhu billah', make wudu, sit if standing, lie down if sitting. The strong person controls anger.

arab: إِنَّ اللَّهَ كَتَبَ الإِحْسَانَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ، فَإِذَا قَتَلْتُمْ فَأَحْسِنُوا الْقِتْلَةَ، وَإِذَا ذَبَحْتُمْ فَأَحْسِنُوا الذَّبْحَ
en: Allah has prescribed excellence in everything. So if you kill, kill well; and if you slaughter, slaughter well.
lesson: Ihsan in everything — even slaughtering animals. Use sharp knife, don't let other animals see, be merciful. Excellence is our way.

arab: اتَّقِ اللَّهَ حَيْثُمَا كُنْتَ، وَأَتْبِعِ السَّيِّئَةَ الْحَسَنَةَ تَمْحُهَا، وَخَالِقِ النَّاسَ بِخُلُقٍ حَسَنٍ
en: Fear Allah wherever you are, follow a bad deed with a good deed to erase it, and treat people with good character.
lesson: 3-step formula: Taqwa + Good deeds erase sins + Good character. Sin? Make tawbah + do good immediately. Don't despair.

arab: يَا غُلاَمُ إِنِّي أُعَلِّمُكَ كَلِمَاتٍ: احْفَظِ اللَّهَ يَحْفَظْكَ، احْفَظِ اللَّهَ تَجِدْهُ تُجَاهَكَ، إِذَا سَأَلْتَ فَاسْأَلِ اللَّهَ، وَإِذَا اسْتَعَنْتَ فَاسْتَعِنْ بِاللَّهِ
en: O young man, I will teach you some words: Be mindful of Allah and He will protect you. Be mindful of Allah and you will find Him before you. If you ask, ask Allah. If you seek help, seek help from Allah.
lesson: Tawakkul. Allah's help is closer than you think. Teach this to kids — full trust in Allah from young age.

arab: إِنَّ مِمَّا أَدْرَكَ النَّاسُ مِنْ كَلاَمِ النُّبُوَّةِ الأُولَى: إِذَا لَمْ تَسْتَحِ فَاصْنَعْ مَا شِئْتَ
en: Among the words people received from the first prophecy: If you feel no shame, then do as you wish.
lesson: Haya is part of Iman. If you have no shame before Allah, you'll do anything. Shame prevents sin. The Prophet ﷺ had more haya than a veiled virgin.

arab: قُلْ: آمَنْتُ بِاللَّهِ، ثُمَّ اسْتَقِمْ
en: Say: 'I believe in Allah,' then remain steadfast.
lesson: Iman + Istiqamah. Believe, then stay firm. Don't be a seasonal Muslim. Consistency in worship even if small.

arab: أَرَأَيْتَ إِذَا صَلَّيْتُ الْمَكْتُوبَاتِ، وَصُمْتُ رَمَضَانَ، وَأَحْلَلْتُ الْحَلاَلَ، وَحَرَّمْتُ الْحَرَامَ، وَلَمْ أَزِدْ عَلَى ذَلِكَ شَيْئًا، أَأَدْخُلُ الْجَنَّةَ؟ قَالَ: نَعَمْ
en: Tell me, if I pray the obligatory prayers, fast Ramadan, consider lawful what is lawful and unlawful what is unlawful, and add nothing to that, will I enter Paradise? He said: Yes.
lesson: Fara'id get you to Jannah. Nafl gets you Allah's love. Don't neglect obligatory acts chasing extra ones.

arab: الطُّهُورُ شَطْرُ الإِيمَانِ، وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ تَمْلأُ الْمِيزَانَ، وَسُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ تَمْلآنِ مَا بَيْنَ السَّمَاءِ وَالأَرْضِ
en: Purity is half of faith. 'Alhamdulillah' fills the scales. 'Subhan Allah' and 'Alhamdulillah' fill what is between heaven and earth.
lesson: Wudu, dhikr, prayer, sabr, Qur'an — your complete spiritual program. These simple words are heavy on the scales.

arab: يَا عِبَادِي إِنِّي حَرَّمْتُ الظُّلْمَ عَلَى نَفْسِي وَجَعَلْتُهُ بَيْنَكُمْ مُحَرَّمًا فَلاَ تَظَالَمُوا
en: O My servants, I have forbidden oppression for Myself and have made it forbidden among you, so do not oppress one another.
lesson: Hadith Qudsi. Allah never wrongs anyone. So we must never wrong anyone. Zulm = darkness on Judgment Day.

arab: أَنَّ نَاسًا مِنْ أَصْحَابِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ قَالُوا لِلنَّبِيِّ ﷺ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ذَهَبَ أَهْلُ الدُّثُورِ بِالأُجُورِ... قَالَ: أَوَلَيْسَ قَدْ جَعَلَ اللَّهُ لَكُمْ مَا تَصَّدَّقُونَ
en: Some companions said: 'O Messenger of Allah, the rich have taken all the rewards...' He said: 'Has Allah not made for you ways to give charity? Every tasbih is charity, every takbir is charity.'
lesson: Poor? You can still give sadaqah. Smile, dhikr, helping others — all charity. Allah's doors of good are endless.

arab: كُلُّ سُلاَمَى مِنَ النَّاسِ عَلَيْهِ صَدَقَةٌ كُلَّ يَوْمٍ تَطْلُعُ فِيهِ الشَّمْسُ: تَعْدِلُ بَيْنَ الاِثْنَيْنِ صَدَقَةٌ
en: Charity is due upon every joint of a person every day the sun rises: Acting justly between two people is charity.
lesson: 360 joints = 360 charities daily. 2 rak'ahs of Duha covers all of them. Or help someone, remove harm from path — all sadaqah.

arab: الْبِرُّ حُسْنُ الْخُلُقِ، وَالإِثْمُ مَا حَاكَ فِي صَدْرِكَ وَكَرِهْتَ أَنْ يَطَّلِعَ عَلَيْهِ النَّاسُ
en: Righteousness is good character, and sin is what wavers in your heart and you hate for people to know about.
lesson: Your fitrah knows. If your heart feels uneasy and you hide it from people, it's probably sin. Good character = Jannah.

arab: أُوصِيكُمْ بِتَقْوَى اللَّهِ، وَالسَّمْعِ وَالطَّاعَةِ وَإِنْ تَأَمَّرَ عَلَيْكُمْ عَبْدٌ، فَإِنَّهُ مَنْ يَعِشْ مِنْكُمْ فَسَيَرَى اخْتِلاَفًا كَثِيرًا
en: I advise you to fear Allah, listen and obey even if a slave is placed in authority over you. Whoever lives among you will see much differing.
lesson: Hold to the Sunnah of the Prophet ﷺ and the Rightly Guided Caliphs. Bite onto it with your molars. Beware of new matters.

arab: أَلاَ أُخْبِرُكَ بِرَأْسِ الأَمْرِ وَعَمُودِهِ وَذِرْوَةِ سَنَامِهِ؟... رَأْسُ الأَمْرِ الإِسْلاَمُ، وَعَمُودُهُ الصَّلاَةُ، وَذِرْوَةُ سَنَامِهِ الْجِهَادُ
en: Shall I not tell you of the head of the matter, its pillar, and its peak? The head of the matter is Islam, its pillar is prayer, and its peak is jihad.
lesson: Prayer is the pillar. If it falls, your Islam collapses. And guard your tongue — it throws people in Hell face-first.

arab: إِنَّ اللَّهَ فَرَضَ فَرَائِضَ فَلاَ تُضَيِّعُوهَا، وَحَدَّ حُدُودًا فَلاَ تَعْتَدُوهَا، وَحَرَّمَ أَشْيَاءَ فَلاَ تَنْتَهِكُوهَا
en: Allah has prescribed obligations, so do not neglect them. He has set limits, so do not transgress them. He has prohibited things, so do not violate them.
lesson: Know the red lines. Allah stayed silent on some matters as a mercy — don't ask about them. Follow what's clear.

arab: ازْهَدْ فِي الدُّنْيَا يُحِبَّكَ اللَّهُ، وَازْهَدْ فِيمَا عِنْدَ النَّاسِ يُحِبَّكَ النَّاسُ
en: Be indifferent to the world and Allah will love you. Be indifferent to what people possess and people will love you.
lesson: Zuhd isn't poverty. It's having dunya in your hand, not heart. Don't chase what people have — chase what Allah has.

arab: لاَ ضَرَرَ وَلاَ ضِرَارَ
en: There should be neither harming nor reciprocating harm.
lesson: Golden rule of fiqh. Don't harm others, and don't harm back. Islam removes hardship. If something harms, it's not from Islam.

arab: لَوْ يُعْطَى النَّاسُ بِدَعْوَاهُمْ لاَدَّعَى رِجَالٌ أَمْوَالَ قَوْمٍ وَدِمَاءَهُمْ، لَكِنِ الْبَيِّنَةُ عَلَى الْمُدَّعِي وَالْيَمِينُ عَلَى مَنْ أَنْكَرَ
en: If people were given according to their claims, men would claim the wealth and lives of others. But the burden of proof is on the claimant and the oath is on the one who denies.
lesson: Justice in Islam. Claim something? Bring proof. Accused? You swear you didn't do it. No one is guilty without evidence.

arab: مَنْ رَأَى مِنْكُمْ مُنْكَرًا فَلْيُغَيِّرْهُ بِيَدِهِ، فَإِنْ لَمْ يَسْتَطِعْ فَبِلِسَانِهِ، فَإِنْ لَمْ يَسْتَطِعْ فَبِقَلْبِهِ، وَذَلِكَ أَضْعَفُ الإِيمَانِ
en: Whoever among you sees an evil, let him change it with his hand. If unable, then with his tongue. If unable, then with his heart — and that is the weakest of faith.
lesson: Stop evil if you can, but with wisdom. Hand = authority. Tongue = advice. Heart = hate the sin. Don't be silent about injustice.

arab: لاَ تَحَاسَدُوا، وَلاَ تَنَاجَشُوا، وَلاَ تَبَاغَضُوا، وَلاَ تَدَابَرُوا، وَلاَ يَبِعْ بَعْضُكُمْ عَلَى بَيْعِ بَعْضٍ، وَكُونُوا عِبَادَ اللَّهِ إِخْوَانًا
en: Do not envy one another, do not inflate prices, do not hate one another, do not turn away from one another, do not undercut one another in trade, but be servants of Allah as brothers.
lesson: The Muslim community code. No hasad, no hate, no backbiting. A Muslim is brother to a Muslim — no oppression, no betrayal.

arab: مَنْ نَفَّسَ عَنْ مُؤْمِنٍ كُرْبَةً مِنْ كُرَبِ الدُّنْيَا نَفَّسَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ كُرْبَةً مِنْ كُرَبِ يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ
en: Whoever relieves a believer's distress of this world, Allah will relieve his distress on the Day of Resurrection.
lesson: Help people now, Allah helps you on Qiyamah. Cover a Muslim's faults, Allah covers yours. Knowledge path = Jannah path.

arab: إِنَّ اللَّهَ كَتَبَ الْحَسَنَاتِ وَالسَّيِّئَاتِ ثُمَّ بَيَّنَ ذَلِكَ: فَمَنْ هَمَّ بِحَسَنَةٍ فَلَمْ يَعْمَلْهَا كَتَبَهَا اللَّهُ عِنْدَهُ حَسَنَةً كَامِلَةً
en: Allah decreed good and evil deeds, then explained: Whoever intends a good deed but does not do it, Allah writes it as a complete good deed.
lesson: Hadith Qudsi showing Allah's mercy. Intend good = 1 reward. Do it = 10 to 700. Intend sin but leave it for Allah = 1 reward. How Merciful!

arab: إِنَّ اللَّهَ قَالَ: مَنْ عَادَى لِي وَلِيًّا فَقَدْ آذَنْتُهُ بِالْحَرْبِ، وَمَا تَقَرَّبَ إِلَيَّ عَبْدِي بِشَيْءٍ أَحَبَّ إِلَيَّ مِمَّا افْتَرَضْتُ عَلَيْهِ
en: Allah said: Whoever shows hostility to a friend of Mine, I declare war against him. My servant does not draw near to Me with anything more beloved to Me than what I have made obligatory upon him.
lesson: Hadith Qudsi. Fara'id first, then nafl. Keep doing nafl until Allah loves you. Then He becomes your hearing, sight, hand. Du'a accepted.

arab: إِنَّ اللَّهَ تَجَاوَزَ لِي عَنْ أُمَّتِي الْخَطَأَ وَالنِّسْيَانَ وَمَا اسْتُكْرِهُوا عَلَيْهِ
en: Allah has pardoned my Ummah for mistakes, forgetfulness, and what they are forced to do.
lesson: Huge mercy. Forgot? No sin. Mistake? No sin. Forced? No sin. Islam removes hardship. Allah doesn't punish for what you can't control.

arab: كُنْ فِي الدُّنْيَا كَأَنَّكَ غَرِيبٌ أَوْ عَابِرُ سَبِيلٍ
en: Be in this world as if you were a stranger or a traveler.
lesson: Dunya is not your home. You're just passing through. Travel light. Ibn Umar would say: 'When evening comes, don't expect morning. When morning comes, don't expect evening.'
